name: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program
owner: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program Office (US GSA)
description: FedRAMP is a US government-wide compliance program providing a standardized approach to security authorization for cloud services used by federal agencies. Built on NIST SP 800-53 with FedRAMP-specific baselines (Low, Moderate, High, Li-SaaS). This directory contains the FedRAMP document library — guidance, templates, policies, and reference documents. Each subdirectory is an individual FedRAMP document with its own metadata file.
relationToCloudSecurity: Foundational US federal cloud-security authorization program. Every cloud service provider serving US federal customers operates within this framework.
relationToAISecurity: FedRAMP covers cloud services generally; AI services hosted on FedRAMP-authorized cloud platforms inherit base authorization but may need additional review per agency.

license.notes: FedRAMP-published documents are works of the US government and generally in the public domain.

lifecycle.notes: Continuously updated program. Documents revised on rolling cadence.

current_extraction.notes: Top-level directory. Each FedRAMP document is a subdirectory with its own *-metadata.json. files.txt enumerates the contents; marketplace-*.csv is a snapshot of the FedRAMP Marketplace (authorized cloud services).

notes: FedRAMP namespace overview. Individual documents have per-directory metadata.
